recommendations: add and run a focused MCP smoke-test suite (import
tool registration
and one happy-path call per endpoint) in CI
deduplicate endpoint names in the adapter registry (e.g.
multiple `read`) and add explicit namespacing like `io_read`/`lightcurve_read`/`tpf_read`
reduce surface area by exposing only stable high-value APIs first (search/read/core classes) and gate notebook-only interact tools behind optional dependency checks
enforce argument/return schemas with Pydantic models for every endpoint (including array/table serialization rules and unit/time handling)
add robust error mapping to MCP-friendly structured errors (validation
missing optional deps
network/MAST failures
auth/rate-limit
file-format issues)
introduce offline/recorded fixtures for astroquery/remote calls to make tests deterministic and faster
add contract tests that compare MCP endpoint results to direct Lightkurve Python calls for representative workflows
pin and document compatible Lightkurve/Astropy/Numpy ranges in `mcp_output/requirements.txt` and add a version compatibility matrix in README
split very large modules behind thin MCP facades to avoid leaking complex internals and improve maintainability
add timeout/retry/circuit-breaker settings for network-backed search endpoints with configurable defaults
include health and readiness checks in `start_mcp.py` (dependency/import check and optional remote connectivity probe)
improve observability with structured logs
per-endpoint latency/error metrics
and request IDs
harden security by validating file paths/URLs and limiting arbitrary file access in read-like endpoints
add docs with copy-paste MCP usage examples for top workflows (search→download/read→analyze periodogram)
add performance benchmarks for heavy operations (periodogram/correctors) and consider optional async wrappers for long-running calls
set up pre-commit (ruff/black/isort/mypy) and enforce in CI for plugin code quality
add graceful degradation paths when optional packages (bokeh/pandas/sklearn) are missing
create golden tests for serialization of LightCurve/TargetPixelFile/Periodogram objects to ensure backward-compatible MCP responses
add release/versioning policy for the MCP plugin independent of upstream Lightkurve with changelog and deprecation notes

success_factors: End-to-end workflow status is success with all planned nodes executed (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-based adapter strategy was appropriate for this Python library and had high feasibility confidence (0.91).
MCP plugin startup/health validation passed (healthy service over stdio transport).
Repository structure and core modules were correctly identified, enabling meaningful endpoint generation.
Low intrusiveness risk reduced integration friction with upstream project code.
failure_reasons: Quality gap: original project tests were not executed/passed in this run (reported passed=false with empty details), reducing confidence in behavioral parity.
DeepWiki analysis failed, lowering external architecture-context completeness.
Generated artifact metrics are inconsistent (0 LOC, 0 size, 0 endpoints in one section vs populated endpoint list elsewhere), indicating reporting/instrumentation defects.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully; repository imported via zip fallback with 107 files discovered. No node-level timing provided, but no download error signals.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed successfully; medium complexity and low intrusiveness were established, with useful module/dependency extraction. DeepWiki sub-analysis failed.
node_performance.generation_time: Completed successfully; key MCP files and requirements were produced using import adapter mode.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health checks passed, but substantive functional/contract testing is missing; original project test status is effectively non-validated in this workflow.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Undetermined due to missing telemetry (reported 0 MB). Instrumentation should be fixed before drawing conclusions.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Low-to-moderate expected footprint (few generated files), but reported generated file size=0 is likely a metrics bug.
